author: obsidian-full-calendar-remastered
description: Complete Calendar HUB experience. Work with all your calendars in one place. Analyze your time and take action! - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Jovi Koikkara
manifest.description: Powerful calendar solution for schedules, events, plans, tasks, and notes together inside your vault. Supports local note-based calendars plus remote sync options like Google Calendar, CalDAV, ICS files, Tasks, TaskNotes, and more.
manifest.name: Full Calendar Remastered

name: Full Calendar Remastered

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.13.4

*   **Unified Task Backlog System** ([#275](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/275), [#290](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/290), [#313](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/313))

    *   Reworked backlog into a provider-agnostic architecture with shared scheduling flows.
    *   Supports richer drag-to-schedule and drag-to-unschedule interactions.
    *   Improves date synchronization consistency across task backlogs.

*   **Google Tasks Two-Way Sync** ([#288](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/288))

    *   Added a dedicated Google Tasks provider with first-class settings and lifecycle support.
    *   Supports scheduling, completion updates, and backlog integration.
    *   Extends multi-provider task workflows beyond local Tasks and CalDAV.

*   **Holidays Calendar Provider** ([#281](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/281))

    *   Introduced configurable holidays calendars for regional observances.
    *   Makes long-range planning easier by overlaying holiday context into calendar views.

*   **Embedded Widgets and Dashboard Integrations** ([#281](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/281), [#315](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/315))

    *   Added unified embedded widget architecture for weather, backlog, and analysis-oriented blocks.
    *   Improves note-native dashboard workflows with reusable codeblock rendering.
    *   Includes updated embed documentation and showcase assets.

*   **Weather Forecast Expansion** ([#288](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/288), [#294](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/294))

    *   Expanded weather integration UX and reliability.
    *   Strengthened caching/offline behavior and improved forecast presentation.

*   **External Recurrence Overrides + Reminder Sync** ([#298](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/298))

    *   Improved recurring-instance override behavior for remote providers.
    *   Better synchronization of reminder semantics when recurring instances are mutated.

*   **Linked Notes + Template Workflow Refinements** ([#289](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/289), [#304](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/304), [#307](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/307))

    *   Fixed startup hydration race conditions and duplicate note creation edge cases.
    *   Improved recurrence-linked note behavior and frontmatter consistency.
    *   Expanded template-driven note generation and documentation.

*   **Developer API & Local Server Enhancements** ([#315](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/315))

    *   Improved API integration pathways and local-server foundation.
    *   Added stronger credential handling primitives for automation flows.

*   **Provider and Sync Reliability Fixes** ([#314](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/314), [#313](https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/313))

    *   Optimized CalDAV VTODO fetching and synchronization behavior.
    *   Preserved recurrence metadata more safely during updates.
    *   Improved TaskNotes companion date synchronization during reschedules.

*   **Status Bar + Task Placement Fixes**

    *   Resolved all-day/timezone status bar inconsistencies.
    *   Fixed task placement regressions in calendar/backlog flows.

*   **Documentation & Architecture Expansion**

    *   Added/updated comprehensive docs for:
        *   Task backlog architecture
        *   Event filtering and sorting engine
        *   Embedded widgets architecture
        *   Weather integration internals
        *   API integration blueprints
    *   Keeps both user docs and architecture docs aligned with implementation.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.13.2...0.13.3

## Contributors
Thanks to all the wonderful contributions that made this possible.

- @Itrekr - major updates and fixes mainly focused at VTODO (CalDAV) and Task backlogs
- @oscariquelme01 - Queries in Tasks Backlogs


***

## Usage

> For Obsidian Community Guideline reasons, all external dependencies are now bundled, making the `main.js` hefty 7.26MB (\~380ms loadtime). If you prefer a leaner version feel free to shallow clone the repo and build via `pnpm run prod:lean`

1.  Update the plugin via Obsidian or manually replace files in `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calendar/`.
2.  Reload Obsidian to ensure provider registries, reminder systems, and embedded widgets initialize cleanly.
3.  (Optional) Enable or configure:
    *   **Google Tasks** calendar source for task sync
    *   **Holidays** calendar source for regional overlays
    *   **Weather Forecasts** for day/week/month weather context
    *   **Linked Note Templates** for richer event-note generation
    *   **API access** if using local automation workflows
4.  Continue scheduling with improved backlog workflows, stronger recurrence handling, and broader provider coverage.
releases.name: v0.13.4

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.13.2

*   **ChronoAnalyser & Dynamic Asset System**

    *   Introduced ChronoAnalyser demo to improve accessibility and provide insight into upcoming analytical capabilities.
    *   Implemented dynamic i18n and NLP asset loading with remote synchronization.
    *   Added version-based remote asset refresh to ensure users always receive the latest language packs and NLP updates without manual intervention.

*   **Google Calendar Integration**

    *   Added full Google Calendar provider support.
    *   Supports event creation, updates, and synchronization with external calendars.
    *   Expands multi-provider ecosystem alongside existing integrations.

*   **Reactive Linked Notes System**

    *   Introduced automatic linked note creation for calendar events.
    *   Features include:
        *   Template-based note generation
        *   Metadata linking via event UID and calendar ID
        *   Seamless integration across all providers (Google, CalDAV, ICS, Outlook)
        *   Event modal + settings UI controls for managing note behavior
    *   Improves knowledge management workflows and event-note traceability.

*   **Tasks Backlog Global Query Support** (#263, #264)

    *   Added support for Obsidian Tasks global query in the backlog sidebar.
    *   Enhancements include:
        *   Advanced filtering by tags, folders, priority, and regex
        *   Support for inclusion/exclusion rules
        *   Real-time filtering of undated tasks
    *   Enables significantly more powerful and customizable task views.

*   **CalDAV Task (VTODO) Support** (#258)

    *   Extended CalDAV provider to support VTODO tasks.
    *   Improved fetching logic for better reliability and consistency.
    *   Aligns task handling across providers.

*   **Unified Reminder System & FCR Companion Integration**

    *   Centralized reminder logic via the NotificationManager.
    *   Introduced full FCR Reminder Companion integration:
        *   Background synchronization of upcoming reminders (24h window)
        *   Deep-link support back into the vault
        *   Manual sync command and NLP trigger support
        *   Startup liveness checks with retry logic and user warnings
    *   Prevents duplicate notifications and ensures consistent scheduling behavior across environments.

*   **Timezone & ICS Serialization Improvements** (#265)

    *   Improved timezone handling for ICS/CalDAV events.
    *   Enhancements include:
        *   Correct TZID handling for local times
        *   Accurate UTC serialization
        *   Consistent formatting across event types
    *   Reduces cross-provider time inconsistencies.

*   **Event Cache & UI Refresh Improvements** (#262)

    *   Optimized update propagation to avoid unnecessary full calendar reloads.
    *   Ensures partial updates are applied correctly based on affected calendars.
    *   Improves performance and visual stability during sync operations.

*   **Linked Notes Stability Fixes**

    *   Fixed issues with note creation, templating, and frontmatter formatting.
    *   Prevents duplication and ensures consistent metadata structure across notes.

*   **ICS / VTODO Compliance Fixes** (#258, #257)

    *   Corrected due date handling to align with RFC 5545.
    *   Fixes include:
        *   Removal of incorrect ±1 day adjustments
        *   Correct duration handling for DUE-only tasks
        *   Consistent behavior across recurring and non-recurring tasks

*   **Provider & Settings Fixes** (#268)

    *   Resolved issues in Google and Outlook settings state management.
    *   Fixed inconsistencies during calendar configuration and updates.

*   **Instance Initialization Fix**

    *   Added proper listener for instance initialization.
    *   Ensures calendar sources update reliably during startup.

*   **Daily Note Reminder Fix**

    *   Resolved issue where `notify` attributes were incorrectly serialized as object strings.
    *   Ensures correct reminder behavior from daily notes.

*   **Documentation Improvements**

    *   Added:
        *   Timezone architecture clarifications
        *   Event cache “UI Refresh Invariant” deep-dive
        *   FCR reminder integration and architecture docs
        *   Tasks global query usage documentation
    *   Improves both developer and user understanding of system behavior.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.13.1...0.13.2 or better see [here]()


## New Contributors
* @Itrekr made their first contribution in https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/pull/267

***

## Usage

1.  Update the plugin via Obsidian or manually replace files in `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calendar/`.
2.  Reload Obsidian to ensure all new systems (especially reminders, providers, and linked notes) initialize correctly.
3.  (Optional) Enable:
    *   Linked Notes in settings to automatically create event notes
    *   FCR Reminder Companion for background reminder synchronization
    *   Tasks global query option for enhanced backlog filtering
4.  Continue using your calendars with improved reliability, richer integrations, and expanded feature support.
releases.name: v0.13.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.13.1

*   **Provider Lifecycle & Stability Improvements**

    *   Introduced optional `teardown` method for CalendarProvider to allow proper cleanup of provider resources.
    *   Ensured providers are safely torn down before:
        *   registry reinitialization
        *   application shutdown
    *   Prevents stale state, memory leaks, and inconsistent provider behavior during reloads.

*   **TaskNotes Synchronization Overhaul**

    *   Stabilized provider-driven updates and drag/toggle synchronization flows.
    *   Improvements include:
        *   Ignoring file watcher updates when no provider claims the file
        *   Recovering missing provider-session mappings from cache store
        *   Normalizing TaskNotes persistent IDs across update paths
        *   Coalescing burst updates to reduce redundant processing
        *   Ignoring stale or out-of-order update payloads
        *   Preferring canonical cache state during reconciliation
        *   Ensuring scheduled changes remain authoritative even if time estimate persistence fails
    *   Significantly improves reliability under rapid or concurrent updates.

*   **CalDAV Recurring Sync Fixes** (#260)

    *   Resolved sync-key collisions for recurring events using RRULE.
    *   Updated sync strategy to use recurring event IDs instead of UID alone.
    *   Prevents conflicts between:
        *   series parent events
        *   RECURRENCE-ID exception instances sharing the same UID
    *   Added regression test covering:
        *   yearly RRULE series
        *   exception instances from CalDAV ICS data

*   **Cache & Update Queue Reliability Improvements**

    *   Fixed `clearUpdateQueue` method in CacheSubscriptionManager.
    *   Improved update flush logic to correctly handle:
        *   recurring child event deletions
        *   burst update scenarios
    *   Ensures more predictable and consistent cache synchronization.

*   **Rendering & Lint Fixes**

    *   Resolved lint-related issues introduced in previous updates.
    *   Improved document handling in:
        *   `getCalendarColors`
        *   `renderCalendar`
    *   Enhances rendering stability and reduces edge case failures.

*   **Documentation Improvements**

    *   Updated specificity-based file ownership model.
    *   Clarified behavior for multiple local providers and overlapping scopes.
    *   Improves developer understanding of provider resolution logic.



**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.13.0...0.13.1

***

## Usage

1.  Update the plugin via Obsidian or manually replace files in `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calendar/`.
2.  Reload Obsidian to ensure provider lifecycle changes take effect.
3.  Continue using your calendars with improved stability, especially for TaskNotes and recurring events.
releases.name: v0.13.1

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.13.0

* **Outlook Calendar Integration (Major Feature)**  

    * Added full Microsoft Outlook calendar integration via a new `OutlookProvider`.
    * Supports secure OAuth2 authentication with PKCE for modern Microsoft account login.
    * Includes `OutlookAuthManager` for token lifecycle management and session handling.
    * Fetch and manage multiple Outlook calendars directly from within the plugin.
    * Configurable Microsoft client settings for advanced/custom setups.
    * Improved error handling and user feedback for authentication and API operations.

* **Outlook Sync Enhancements**  

    * Added support for recurring events in Outlook calendars.
    * Improved API handling and event synchronization consistency.

* **Advanced NLP Parsing Improvements**  

    * Introduced explicit category-first parsing (`category <name>`) at sentence start.
    * Improved fuzzy category matching for typo tolerance (e.g. `wrok` → `Work`).
    * Added support for compact anchored times (e.g. `at 430pm`).
    * Implemented time range parsing (`from 3pm to 5 pm`).
    * Prioritized anchored time triggers (`at`, `from`) to avoid title-number conflicts.
    * Added explicit end-time handling in NLP execution pipeline.
    * Expanded NLP test coverage:
      * compact time formats  
      * time ranges  
      * anchored time vs title collisions  
      * category-first scenarios  

* **Milestones System (New Feature)**  

    * Introduced a complete milestones tracking system for user activity:
      * create, update, delete, and move events  
    * Added milestone definitions and progress computation engine.
    * Integrated milestone tracking into NLP-driven event creation.
    * New `SHOW_MILESTONES` intent to quickly access milestone data.
    * Added `MilestonesModal` UI for viewing progress and achievements.
    * Toast notifications for milestone unlocks.
    * Persistent storage of milestone progress in plugin state.
    * Added Chinese (i18n) support for milestone-related strings.
    * Settings UI now includes quick access to milestones.
    * Custom styling for milestone UI and notifications.

* **Task System Improvements** (#254)  

    * Added deduplication for mirrored tasks to prevent duplicates.
    * Enhanced task interface for better consistency and stability.

* **Frontmatter Reliability Improvements** (#256)  

    * Reworked frontmatter modification logic to preserve formatting.
    * Improved parsing and updates for safer metadata handling.

* **UI & Modal Enhancements**  

    * Added footer to “What’s New” modal.
    * Improved overall UI consistency across new features.

* **Documentation & DX Improvements**  

    * Updated NLP documentation with new syntax and examples:
      * category-first parsing  
      * compact/anchored time formats  
      * time ranges  
    * Expanded troubleshooting guidance for NLP edge cases.
    * Enforced strict hyperlinking standards across documentation.

* **Workflow & Project Maintenance**  

    * Updated stale workflow to automatically close inactive items after 30 days.
    * Updated project metadata to reflect migration to a new organisation.


## New Contributors
* @luyluc made their first contribution in #246 

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/obsidian-full-calendar-remastered/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.9...0.13.0

-----

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calendar/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2. Open Full Calendar and connect your providers (including Outlook if enabled).
3. Enjoy smarter NLP parsing, milestone tracking, and expanded calendar integrations.
releases.name: v0.13.0

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.9

* **ActivityWatch Integration (Major Upgrade)**  

    * Introduced a full-featured ActivityWatch sync engine for automatic time tracking → calendar events.
    * Supports flexible rule-based filtering across any ActivityWatch buckets (browser, editor, window, AFK, etc.).
    * Dynamic title templating (e.g. `{app}`, `{title}`, `{url}`, `{file}`) for rich, contextual event naming.

* **Smarter Session Detection (FSM Engine)**  

    * Rebuilt sync logic using a multi-phase state machine (Idle → Warmup → Active).
    * Improved detection of meaningful work sessions while filtering short distractions and noise.
    * Added primary vs supporting evidence rules for more precise and customizable session behavior.

* **Continuity-Aware Sync & Auto-Sync**  

    * Events now intelligently extend across sync intervals instead of fragmenting.
    * Continuity-safe replacement logic prevents duplicates and preserves correct timelines.
    * Added configurable auto-sync scheduling with quiet background execution.

* **Massive Performance Improvements**  

    * Replaced expensive full rebuild sync with a fast identity-diff algorithm (O(N)).
    * Reduced large-vault sync times from seconds to milliseconds.
    * Optimized overlap detection and event matching with sweep-line and two-pointer algorithms.

* **Daily Note Event Identity Overhaul**  

    * Replaced fragile title-based deduplication with persistent `[uid:: N]` identifiers.
    * Event titles can now be safely edited without breaking sync or causing duplicates.
    * Faster and more reliable event updates.

* **Improved Reliability & Data Integrity**  

    * Fixed duplicate event generation during long-running sessions.
    * Added safeguards for providers that cannot safely delete events.
    * Resolved metadata race conditions during startup to prevent missing events.

* **Settings & UI Enhancements**  

    * Redesigned settings with tabs, search, and improved descriptions.
    * Expanded ActivityWatch configuration UI with rule editors and profile controls.
    * Calendar improvements:
      * Current/next event highlighting
      * Touch + keyboard navigation
      * Better responsive behavior

* **Search & Navigation Improvements**  

    * Enhanced toolbar search UX and discoverability.
    * Fixed search duplication bugs.
    * Added icons and improved navigation interactions.

* **Bundle Size & Startup Optimizations**  

    * Reduced bundle size via dynamic i18n loading.
    * Faster initialization (~170 ms startup).
    * Improved build pipeline using Preact optimizations.

* **Documentation & Architecture Transparency**  

    * Added comprehensive ActivityWatch documentation and architecture guides.
    * Expanded feature docs with FAQs and setup instructions.
    * Reorganized documentation for easier navigation.

* **Tasks Integration Enhancements** (#142, #166, #175)  

    * Improved Tasks integration with better payload handling and retry logic.
    * Added backlog integration and calendar display configuration options.
    * Enhanced event handling and cache warm-up behavior.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.7.1...0.12.9

    
-----

## Usage

1.  Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calendar/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2.  Open the Full Calendar plugin and load your calendar as usual.
3.  Enjoy the new features, improved syncing, and enhanced timezone stability.
releases.name: v0.12.9

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.8

  * **Tasks Time Features** (#227)  

      * Tasks can now have an optional time block.
      * Added drag-drop task time block updates. Dragging to a time updates the start time; dragging to all-day removes it.

  * **CalDAV enhancements** (#230)  

      * Auto-fetch calendar name and color when importing a collection.
      * Added correct validation for imported collections.

  * **Advanced Categorization enhancements** (#222, #231) 

      * Introduced more robust advanced categorization workflows.
      * Added a "Disable without cleanup" option to the advanced categorization modal.

  * **Robust timezone handling for recurring/all-day events**  (#194, #223, #231)  

      * Timezone handling has been significantly hardened.
      * Prevented `RRULE TZID` one-day date shift when recurring.
      * Prevented timezone shifts for all-day, date-only events (floating dates).

  * **Provider file sync refactor** (#224)

      * Refactored `ProviderRegistry` file delete handling during rename races.
      * Fixed `FullNoteProvider` rename logic to avoid `ENOENT` errors.

  * **Remote Payload Hardening** (#218)  

      * Handled null-body statuses to prevent ghost syncing issues.
      * Added fast-fail logic for malformed CalDAV XML payloads.

  * **UI, Workspaces & Localization**

      * Improved desktop and mobile UI responsiveness.
      * Workspace calendar filters now display user-defined calendar names, making it easier to distinguish between multiples of the same source.
      * Updated ES, FR, and IT translations.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.7.1...0.12.8

## Contributors
* Thanks to @oskardotglobal and @rolfkleef for their contributions in this release!

-----

## Usage

1.  Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calendar/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2.  Open the Full Calendar plugin and load your calendar as usual.
3.  Enjoy the new features, improved syncing, and enhanced timezone stability.
releases.name: v0.12.8

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.7.1

* **Massive performance overhaul (5x faster overall)**
  * Core event synchronization engine fully rewritten for speed and responsiveness.
  * Eliminates 1–3 second UI freezes previously occurring during startup.
  * Total two-stage discovery reduced from ~4.9s → ~0.9s on large datasets.

* **True asynchronous event discovery pipeline**
  * Remote providers now use a real Stage 1 → Stage 2 pipeline.
  * Stage 2 begins immediately after its provider’s Stage 1 completes (no global Promise barrier).
  * Remote calendars start loading earlier in the background for improved perceived performance.

* **Parallelized local provider loading**
  * Local Stage 1 reads now use `Promise.all` instead of sequential loops.
  * Significantly improves vault scan throughput.
  * Daily Note Stage 1: ~1209ms → ~111ms (~11x faster).
  * Daily Note Stage 2: ~2839ms → ~143ms (~20x faster).

* **Event cache diff optimization**
  * Removed expensive O(N) `JSON.stringify` diffing when cache size changes significantly.
  * Added short-circuit invalidation logic for obvious batch replacements.
  * Prevents unnecessary heavy comparisons during Stage 2 merges.

* **Selective UI reconciliation (no more full teardown)**
  * Replaced `removeAllEventSources()` with targeted updates.
  * UI now refreshes only affected calendars via an `affectedCalendars` array.
  * Eliminates massive DOM rebuild cycles and resolves startup freezing.

* **Daily Note O(N²) algorithm fix**
  * Removed full vault iteration from `getEventHandle`.
  * Events now resolve by date string instead of scanning the entire Daily Note cache.
  * `ProviderRegistry` injects authoritative `location.path` metadata before write/delete operations.
  * Dramatically reduces overhead for large Daily Note collections.

* **Improved code compliance & type safety**
  * Enforced ESLint UI string formatting rules.
  * Strengthened TypeScript type contracts across components.
  * Refactored core modules for safer long-term maintainability.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.7...0.12.7.1

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and load your calendar as usual.
3. Enjoy dramatically faster startup and smoother UI performance.
releases.name: v0.12.7.1

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.7

* **Faster startup with staged loading**
  * Calendar now loads in two stages for improved responsiveness.
  * Stage 1 loads the critical date range (±3 months) for immediate interaction.
  * Stage 2 loads full history in the background.
  * Prevents UI freezes when remote calendars (CalDAV/Google) are slow or timing out.

* **Improved timezone & DST reliability** (#194)
  * Major hardening of timezone handling for single and recurring events.
  * Fixes time drift across DST transitions (EU/US and non-hour offset regions).
  * Recurring events now preserve correct local times across DST boundaries.
  * Better handling of EXDATEs and recurrence rules for consistent behavior.

* **Local ICS file support**
  * ICS calendars can now be loaded directly from files in your vault.
  * Toggle between Remote URL and Local File in the ICS settings.
  * Useful for offline workflows or exported calendars.

* **Self-Hosted LiveSync compatibility fix** (#169)
  * Fixes calendar rendering issue caused by DOM modifications from LiveSync.
  * Ensures the calendar always renders in the correct container.

* **Mobile & UI improvements** (#191)
  * Improved mobile styling for FullCalendar views.
  * Better layout and responsiveness in modals and settings.
  * Cleaner labels and improved visual consistency.

* **Improved remote calendar resilience**
  * Remote sync now runs in the background to prevent blocking Obsidian.
  * Local calendars load immediately, even if remote feeds are slow.

* **General stability & internal improvements**
  * Removed legacy dependencies from timezone handling.
  * Improved error handling and logging.
  * Significant internal type-safety and lint cleanup for long-term stability.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.6.2...0.12.7

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!
releases.name: v0.12.7

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
What’s New in v0.12.6.2

- Lint fixes for Obsidian Community plugin guidelines

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.6...0.12.6.2
releases.name: v0.12.6.2

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.6

* **Full CalDAV two-way sync** (#205)
  * CalDAV calendars are no longer read-only: create, edit, and delete events directly in Obsidian.
  * Changes now sync back to your CalDAV server.
  * Rich read-only event modal shows descriptions, attendees, and other details.

* **Mobile workspace support** (#203)
  * Workspaces are now accessible on mobile and narrow layouts.
  * Added a workspace button to the footer toolbar.
  * Improved menu handling and truncated labels for better fit.
  * Renamed the button from `WS ▾` to `Workspace ▾` for clarity.

* **Mobile monthly view** (#201)
  * Monthly view is now available on mobile via the view dropdown or workspace settings.

* **ICS parsing and date validation hardening** (#199, #203)
  * Maps Windows timezones (e.g., `W. Europe Standard Time`) to IANA, fixing Outlook/Exchange calendars falling back to UTC.
  * Converts `YYYYMMDD` dates to ISO and improves handling of all-day events and skip dates in recurrences.
  * Adds defensive checks to prevent `Invalid time value` crashes, with better error logging.

* **Google Calendar settings fix** (#204)
  * Credentials menu now displays correctly in the Google Calendar settings.

* **Title and category parsing improvements** (#203)
  * Prevents false category matches (e.g., “anything - Red Hat One”).
  * Displays the full title when no category is defined.


## New Contributors
* @mivanit made their first contribution in https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/pull/204
* @kapej42 made their first contribution in https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/pull/199

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.5...0.12.6

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!

## What's Changed
* fix (add) gcal settings credentials menu by @mivanit in https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/pull/204
* Fix ICS Date Parsing by @kapej42 in https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/pull/199
* Feat by @YouFoundJK in https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/pull/205
releases.name: v0.12.6

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.5

* **CalDAV validation and parsing** (#193)  
  - Validates calendar collection URLs via PROPFIND (no more regex guesses).  
  - Parses `calendar-data` with `DOMParser`, falling back to per-ICS fetches when needed.  
  - JSDOM-backed tests for `CalDAVProvider` and parsing helpers.  
  - Clearer errors when a URL isn’t a calendar collection.

* **Provider initialization and cache refresh** (#173)  
  - Providers call `initialize()` after creation.  
  - New `onAllComplete` callbacks and cache-aware loading keep the UI in sync.  
  - Resync refreshes event sources without a full calendar rebuild.  
  - Load priorities adjusted for Daily Note and Tasks providers.

* **Google auth and recurring timezone handling** (#191, #190, #94)  
  - Mobile OAuth opens a placeholder tab first to avoid popup blockers.  
  - Recurring events respect source timezones for BYDAY alignment.  
  - “Fake UTC” exdates hide deleted instances correctly across DST.  
  - Improved debug logging for tricky recurrence cases.

* **Obsidian Bases provider**  
  - Add Bases as a read-only calendar source, with a guard that prompts you to enable the Bases plugin before linking.

* **ChronoAnalyser data integrity**  
  - Uses the main EventStore (not the test store).  
  - Correctly parses category/project/subproject from titles for accurate analysis.

* **General maintenance**  
  - UI resync polish and safer cache population paths.  
  - Docs updated with new workflows and Bases setup.  
  - Tests updated; lint/compile clean.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.4...0.12.5

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!
releases.name: v0.12.5

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.4

* **Refactored CalDAV Provider** (#164)
  The CalDAV integration has been fully rewritten for improved reliability, security, and compatibility:

  * Removed `tsdav` dependency in favor of direct CalDAV REPORT/GET requests with custom XML.
  * Added `helper_caldav.ts` for URL parsing, normalization, and collection validation.
  * Introduced strict import logic: only explicit collection URLs are accepted (no `.well-known` or root endpoints).
  * Added Obsidian-compatible fetch utility for CalDAV requests.
  * Refactored `CalDAVConfigComponent` and `CalDAVProvider` for simplified setup, better error handling, and clearer UI guidance.

* **Calendar Source Enhancements** (#170)

  * Added a **`name` field** to all calendar source types (local, dailynote, and ical).
  * Updated settings UI to support renaming calendars directly.
  * Migration ensures existing sources receive default names.
  * Simplified and unified name handling logic across the plugin.

* **FullNoteProvider Reliability Improvements** (#176)

  * Now supports **multiple events with the same title**.
  * Enforces **unique file paths** for all new events.
  * Ensures **event UIDs are consistently tied to file paths**.
  * Excludes UID from written frontmatter for cleaner files.
  * Removed legacy debug and commented code from core modules.

* **Internationalization (i18n) and Multi-language Support** (#183)
  Full multi-language support powered by **i18next**:

  * Added English, German, French, Italian, and Spanish translations.
  * Replaced all hardcoded UI strings with translation keys.
  * Updated settings, modals, and command palette for full localization.
  * Added automatic Obsidian language detection and i18n initialization.
  * Comprehensive test coverage for translation consistency.

* **Task & Backlog Fixes** (#175, #143)

  * Fixed issue where tasks dropped from the backlog column incorrectly opened the edit modal.
  * Completed tasks now correctly add an `x` to the checkbox.

* **General Maintenance & Cleanup**
  * Gramatical fix (#172). Thanks to @Happypig375
  * Updated internal documentation and Copilot instructions.
  * Minor UI polish and performance improvements across modules.
  * All tests passing (except known timezone test issue).

### New Contributors
* @Happypig375 made their first contribution in https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/pull/172

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.3...0.12.4

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory. Reload the Obsidian App.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!
releases.name: v0.12.4

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/). It is recommended to backup your local Calendar Sources as well as data.json file before updating!

### What’s New in v0.12.3.1-beta

#### **Deep Tasks Integration 2.0** (#143, #144, #151, #155)

  A complete overhaul of the **TasksProvider** brings real-time syncing, surgical editing, and better reliability when working with Obsidian Tasks.

* **Live Cache Integration** — Tasks are now synced directly from the Tasks plugin’s internal cache via an event-driven system.
* **Surgical Editing** — Updates only the relevant parts of each line, preserving user metadata like links, tags, and comments, with much of it being delegated to the Tasks UI via its API.
* **Improved Drag-and-Drop** — Moving tasks between days or from the backlog now correctly updates scheduled dates and triggers the edit modal. Rail guard checks ensure the `scheduleDate` makes sense.
* **Provider-Specific Completion Logic** — `toggleComplete` now supports per-provider behavior.
* **Tasks Creation NOT SUPPORTED** — Creating tasks plugin compactible events within FCR has been depreciated in favor of having a more robust and maintainable integration. Of course you can create events in the standard Tasks plugin worflow and those will show up in the Calendar UI!

#### **Settings & Calendar UI Enhancements** (#141)

The settings interface has been refactored for stability, consistency, and better maintainability.

* Introduced a new **`CalendarSettingRow`** component for unified layout across providers.
* Fixed **color picker focus loss** when selecting colors.
* Updated `ProviderAwareCalendarSettingRow` to delegate provider-specific settings as children.
* Removed deprecated `ChromeWrapper`.

#### **ICS Provider Improvements** (#91)

* Added **timezone fallback to UTC** when no valid timezone match is found, improving compatibility with external calendar feeds.

#### **Codebase Refactor & Structure Modernization**

A large-scale reorganization for improved clarity and modularity.

* Migrated to a **feature-based directory structure** (`features/category`, `features/navigation`, etc.).
* Removed obsolete utilities (`TasksParser.ts`, `markdown.ts`, `splitter.ts`) and legacy tests.
* Replaced old test suites with new focused unit tests.
* Moved `overrides.css` to a more logical location under `calendar/styles`.

#### **Documentation Updates**

* Updated **TasksProvider documentation** with new architecture details.
* Changed “Suggest new feature” link to the GitHub Discussions page for broader community feedback.

#### **Maintenance & Cleanup**

* Removed debug logs and redundant code.
* Reworked old integration tests for modern provider flow.
* General quality-of-life improvements and consistency fixes.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.3...0.12.3.1-beta

---

## Usage

> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/). It is recommended to backup your local Calendar Sources as well as data.json file before updating!

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.12.3

* **Deep Tasks Integration with Backlog & Filtering** (#122, #128, #136)  
  Full Calendar now has first-class support for the Obsidian Tasks plugin:  
  * New “Tasks” calendar source syncs tasks directly into the calendar.  
  * Dedicated backlog sidebar with drag-and-drop scheduling.  
  * Create, update, complete, and delete tasks directly from the calendar.  

* **Advanced Date Navigation** (#106)  
  Navigate with precision using the new **“Go To”** dropdown in the toolbar:  
  * Context-aware shortcuts (“This Month”, “This Week”).  
  * Flatpickr date picker for exact jumps.  
  * Right-click on calendar dates to switch between month/week/day views.  

* **Improved Task Parsing & Display** (#132, #134, #138)  
  * Support for dated and undated tasks, multi-day events, and custom status detection.  
  * Global filter support for including/excluding tasks.  
  * Optional tag stripping for cleaner task titles.  
  * Comprehensive parsing test coverage for reliability.  

* **Performance & Stability Enhancements**  
  * Surgical updates on file changes (no more full-vault rescans).  
  * Remote calendars load asynchronously in priority order.  
  * Large vaults now update smoothly without lag.  

* **Notification Reliability**  
  End-time reminders now fire correctly for events already in progress, ensuring both start and end alerts work as expected.  

* **Calendar Source Improvements** (#100, #101, #126)  
  * New calendars appear instantly without “Provider not found” flicker.  
  * Daily Notes calendar now works even without a template.  
  * Fixed race condition when removing event sources.  

 
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.2...0.12.3

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/). It is recommended to backup your local Calendar Sources as well as data.json file before updating!

### What’s New in v0.12.2

* **Multi‑Day Daily Note Events**  
  Daily Note provider now supports true multi‑day events using an inline field:  
  `[endDate:: YYYY-MM-DD]`  
  Backward compatible: legacy “overnight” (start > end) still works and is auto‑interpreted.

* **Central TimeEngine**  
  A single lightweight scheduler maintains a sorted time‑sensitive cache and emits `time-tick` events. Consumers (status bar, notifications, future live features) subscribe instead of spawning their own timers.  
  * Predictable lifecycle (owned by `EventCache`).  
  * Eliminates duplicate intervals and drift.  
  * Extensible for future “next transition” logic.

* **Status Bar Integration**  
  Compact now/next event indicator powered by `TimeEngine` (no polling). Hover for quick context; updates instantly on cache or tick changes.

* **Interactive Time‑Axis Zoom (Ctrl / Cmd + Scroll)** (#96)  
  Smooth zoom on:  
  * `timeGrid` views (vertical axis)  
  * `resourceTimeline` (horizontal axis)  
  Respects min/max granular bounds and preserves focus around pointer.

* **Advanced Recurrence Enhancements** (#97)  
  * Interval repeats (e.g. every 2 weeks / every 3 months).  
  * Positional monthly rules: “2nd Tuesday”, “last Friday”, etc.  
  * iCal import/export parity for new rules.  
  * Updated schema + UI controls with validation.

* **Reactive Calendar View Pipeline**  
  Settings diffing now drives targeted refresh:  
  * `view-config-changed` → selective cache repopulate.  
  * New `resync` event → focused re-render without full teardown.  
  * Faster visual feedback; reduced flicker & GC churn.

* **NotificationManager Refactor**  
  Converted from owning its own interval to subscribing to `TimeEngine`. Less overhead, unified timing semantics, easier testing.

* **Robust Cross‑Day Timezone Handling**  
  `convertEvent` rewritten as a pure function:  
  * Handles explicit `endDate` cleanly.  
  * Preserves legacy overnight semantics.  
  * Stricter type guards prevent silent mutation & DST edge regressions.

* **Codebase Compliance & Safety**  
  * Replaced loose `any` casts with precise types.  
  * Moved inline styles into CSS modules.  
  * Added `instanceof` checks for file/folder objects.  
  * Removed custom leaf detaching in `onunload` (Obsidian now manages lifecycle).

* **Internal Quality Improvements**  
  * Cleaner separation of time concerns.  
  * Predictable subscriber pattern for UI + engines.  
  * Lower idle CPU usage with consolidated ticking.
 
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.12.1...0.12.2

---

## Usage

> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/). It is recommended to backup your local Calendar Sources as well as data.json file before updating!

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/). It is recommended to backup your local Calendar Sources as well as data.json file before updating!

### What’s New in v0.12.1

* **Event Reminder System** (BETA):  
  A new `NotificationManager` delivers native desktop reminders. Users can receive alerts 10 minutes before events start (and optionally, before they end).  

* **Provider-Based Architecture & Multi-Account Support**:  
  A major engine overhaul introduces provider-based calendar handling, Google multi-account support, and stronger abstractions.  

  * **Provider Isolation**: Each source (Local, Daily Notes, ICS, CalDAV, Google) is now a self-contained provider with stable Settings IDs.  
  * **ProviderRegistry**: central gateway for all I/O, decoupling persistence from `EventCache`.  
  * **Google Multi-Account**: new `GoogleAuthManager` centralizes authentication, with a redesigned settings hub and a two-step wizard for adding calendars from multiple accounts.  

* **Event-Driven Settings Updates**:  
  Settings persistence has been refactored from a full-reset model to a granular event-driven system for faster, more responsive updates. This improves responsiveness, enforces single responsibility, and makes the settings UI more fluid. 

  * **Main Engine (`main.ts`)**: `saveSettings` now diffs state changes and publishes targeted events (`sources-changed`, `view-config-changed`, `settings-updated`).  
  * **ProviderRegistry**: reloads data only when sources actually change.  
  * **CalendarView**: instantly re-renders on config changes, eliminating flicker.  
  * **Auxiliary Components** (`NotificationManager`, `EventCache`, `EventEnhancer`): subscribe to `settings-updated` for lightweight syncing.  

* **Lazy Loading for Faster Startup**:  
  Heavy dependencies are now loaded only when needed, reducing startup time and memory footprint.  These changes make startup faster and overall plugin performance smoother. 

  * **Calendar Engine (`@fullcalendar/*`)**: dynamically imported on first calendar view.  
  * **React Modals**: modal entry points (`BulkCategorizeModal`, `WorkspaceModal`, `DeleteRecurringModal`, etc.) are now lazy-loaded.  
  * **Core Decoupling**: `main.ts` no longer depends on rendering logic; `ui/view.ts` dynamically imports the calendar renderer; `features/recur_events/RecurringEventManager.ts` lazy-loads its modal.   

* **Centralized Logic & Decoupling**:  
  Cross-cutting responsibilities have been consolidated into focused managers. This improves maintainability and reduces duplication across providers.  

  * **EventEnhancer**: handles timezone conversion and category parsing.  
  * **WorkspaceManager**: manages filtering and workspace-specific display logic.  

* **Performance Optimizations**:  
  Provider modules and the `RecurringEventManager` are now lazily loaded, further reducing startup work and improving responsiveness.  

* **RRULE Timezone Fixes** (#94):  
  Fixed timezone inconsistencies in RRULE branching. Recurring events now correctly include `endDate` handling.  

* **Remote ICS calendars Fixed** (#91):  
  Remote ICS calendars are now parsed correctly.  

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.11.8...0.12.1

---

## Usage

> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/). It is recommended to backup your local Calendar Sources as well as data.json file before updating!

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.11.8

* **Business Hours and Background Events**
  Highlight working hours in calendar views and visually distinguish background events like vacations or focus blocks. Both features are configurable via plugin settings and frontmatter options.

* **Timeline View: Shadow Events**
  Adds support for category "shadow" events in Timeline View against categories for quick overview.

* **Reorganized Settings UI with Footer**
  The settings modal has been streamlined for clarity with grouped sections, tooltip hints, and a new footer for version info and helpful links.

* **Fixes for Recurring Task Timing**
  Undoing completed recurring tasks now correctly preserves custom timing on child events, ensuring accuracy across repeated schedules.

* **All-Day Recurring Events Now Float Correctly**
  All-day events now behave as "floating" in recurrence rules, preventing unintentional time shifts or misalignment.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.11.6...0.11.8

---

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!

releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.11.7

* **Google Calendar Integration with Two-Way Sync**
  Connect your Google account to create, edit, and delete single or recurring events directly from Obsidian. Includes OAuth 2.0 authentication and calendar selection. [Check here](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/calendars/gcal/) on how to set it up.

* **Refined Settings UI and Changelog View**
  The settings tab has been reorganized into clearer sections with improved type safety, and a new changelog component has been added to display updates.

* **Improved Event Parsing and Caching**
  Event parsing now uses a unified enhancement pipeline, and caching is more reliable thanks to modularized update managers.

* **Bug Fixes for Daily Note Parsing and Cache Updates**
  Fixed issues with daily note event parsing and improved cache update logic so changes in frontmatter are correctly reflected.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.11.6...0.11.7

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!
releases.name: v0.11.7

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.11.6

* **Advanced Categorization with Hierarchical Timeline View**
  Events can now be grouped by categories and sub-categories in a new Resource Timeline view. Expandable parent groups and aggregated summary rows improve organization and provide clearer scheduling insights.

* **Drag-and-Drop Category Reassignment**
  You can now change an event’s category or sub-category by dragging it between lanes in the timeline view. Titles and metadata update automatically to reflect the new classification.

* **Cleaner UI and New Initial View Options**
  The event modal and settings have been refined with dropdown controls and a new option to select the timeline view as the initial calendar display.

* **Improved Event Titles and Filenames**
  Events display clearer titles combining sub-category and event name (e.g., “SubCategory - Event Name”), while internal data and filenames stay consistent and clean.

* **Fixed Multi-Level Category Parsing**
  Parsing of event titles with multiple category levels (e.g., “Category - SubCategory - Title”) has been corrected, ensuring accurate assignment of category and sub-category fields.

* **License Updated to GPLv3**
  The plugin license is now GPLv3 to comply with FullCalendar’s licensing requirements.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.11.4...0.11.6

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract `obsidian-full-calender-chronotask-plugin_0.11.4.7z` into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!
releases.name: v0.11.6

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/)

### What’s New in v0.11.5-beta

* **Monthly & Yearly Recurring Events** (#44):
  You can now create events that repeat every month or every year — great for anniversaries, seasonal reviews, or yearly deadlines. This extends the recurrence system beyond weekly events, with native FullCalendar rendering support.

* **New "Repeats" Dropdown UI**:
  The old recurring checkbox has been replaced with a flexible "Repeats" menu: `None`, `Weekly`, `Monthly`, `Yearly`. The form dynamically adapts to show the appropriate options for each type — e.g., weekday picker, day-of-month, or month selector.

* **Smart Recurrence Handling**:
  The recurrence fields are now mutually exclusive, and the system automatically prevents accidental data corruption when switching between recurrence types. Daily Note calendars do not support recurrence options (intentionally disabled for clarity).

* **Improved RRULE Generation**:
  RRULE strings are now correctly generated for all recurrence types:

  * `FREQ=MONTHLY;BYMONTHDAY=...`
  * `FREQ=YEARLY;BYMONTH=...;BYMONTHDAY=...`
    These rules power accurate rendering across FullCalendar and external integrations.

* **Descriptive Filenames for Recurring Events**:
  Recurring Full Note events now generate more readable and meaningful filenames like `(Every year on July 30th) My Event.md` or `(Every month on the 15th) Team Check-in.md`.

* **Timezone & All-Day Improvements**:
  Timezone parsing and rendering has been improved, including support for `skipDate` and proper display of recurring all-day events across different time zones.

* **Recurring Task Fixes**:
  Right-click task toggling now works properly for recurring task instances. This brings task behavior in line with expectations and makes it easier to complete individual instances.

* **Bug Fixes & Maintenance**:

  * Fixed RRULE inconsistencies across timezones
  * Removed unsafe HTML usage from UI rendering
  * UI improvements to loading indicators and input safety
  * General cleanup of deprecated code paths and debug flags

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.11.4...0.11.5

---

## Usage

> Beta version is untested - know the data integrity risks here: [Data Integrity and File Modifications](https://youfoundjk.github.io/plugin-full-calendar/advanced/data_integrity/)

2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!
releases.name: v0.11.5.1

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.11.4

* **Recurring Task Completion** (#30): You can now check off individual instances of recurring tasks without affecting the entire series. Each completed instance becomes its own override note, with changes safely stored and reversible.

* **"Skip and Override" Editing Model**: Dragging or modifying a recurring event instance creates a single-event override and skips the original. Deleting the override restores the original instance. This brings the plugin in line with modern calendar UX.

* **Series Confirmation Modals**: Editing or deleting recurring events now prompts you with options like *"Promote Overrides to Single Events"* or *"Entire series"* — ensuring clear intent and data safety.

* **Global Event ID Infrastructure**: Each calendar now has a persistent, human-readable ID (e.g. `local_1`). Recurring events and overrides are reliably linked across sessions using internal global identifiers.

* **Recurring Task UX Enhancements**:

  * `isTask` now supported for recurring `FullNote` events | disabled for `DailyNote` events
  * Instance-level checkboxes for recurring tasks
  * Inherited values from master events now displayed with helpful tooltips

* **Multiday All-Day Events Fix**: Local calendar support for multi-day all-day events has been corrected. (#33)
  *Thanks to @yalikebaz for the contribution!*

* **Architectural Refactors**:

  * Extracted recurring logic into a dedicated `RecurringEventManager`
  * Separated UI from core logic for better modularity
  * Reorganized form inputs and modals into reusable components

* **Bug Fixes & Polish**:

  * Timezone & DST fixes in RRULE processing
  * Safeguards added for edge cases like orphaned overrides
  * UI cleanup in Edit Event modal
  * Silent update queue for performance optimization
  * Task state now properly inherited in child overrides

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/0.11.3...0.11.4

---

## Usage

1. Download and extract `obsidian-full-calender-chronotask-plugin_0.11.4.7z` into your vault under the `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/` directory.
2. Open the Full Calendar plugin and click the **Analysis** button in the top-right corner to access Chronotask.
3. Explore the new features under plugin settings and enjoy the improved experience!
releases.name: v0.11.4

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.11.3

* **Expandable Nested Insights**: Insight items can now include expandable sub-insights for deeper analysis and better structure.
* **Smarter Filtering**: Use exclusion keywords to mute low-priority activities instead of hiding them. Muted items are now visually de-emphasized and sorted lower.
* **Pro Tips Panel**: A rotating tips panel now appears in the insights view, offering helpful guidance and feature discovery.
* **Persona-Based Grouping**: Insights are now grouped by user-defined personas for clearer context and relevance.
* **Global Category Filter**: Quickly filter all insights by category across the interface.
* **FullNote to DailyNote Converter**: New Python script converts calendar notes to a daily note format with structured markdown output.
* **Fixes & Polish**:

  * Improved handling of corrupted insight configs
  * Unique IDs for TimeRecords
  * UI/UX refinements and layout improvements
  * Debug logs cleaned up for production
  * all day not appearing in calendar by @yalikebaz in #26 
  * fixing off by one error with multi day events by @yalikebaz in #27 

### New Contributors
* @yalikebaz made their first contribution in https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/pull/26

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/v0.11.2...v0.11.3

---

## Usage
1. Download and extract obsidian-full-calender-chronotask-plugin_0.10.8.7z in your vault under .obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/ directory.
2. If you prefer the original feel and look of Chronotask, replace the extracted styles.css with styles_original.css!
3. Finally Open the Full-Calender-plugin and you should see an Analysis button on the top right - use it to access Chronotask; Or open the plugin settings to see latest options.
4. Enjoy!
releases.name: v0.11.3

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
## What’s New in v0.11.2

* **Chrono Insights Engine**: Discover patterns in your data with configurable rules and interactive insight cards.
* **Insight Wizard**: Easily define custom categories (e.g. projects, habits) for smarter analysis.
* **Click-to-Filter Cards**: Instantly apply filters by clicking on insight cards.
* **Performance Boosts**: Non-blocking analysis, faster UI, and smoother chart rendering.
* **UI Refresh**: Cleaner layout, better modals, and improved usability.
* **Bug Fixes & Stability**: Category color bugs, memory leaks, and startup layout issues resolved.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/v0.11.1...v0.11.2

---

### Usage
1. Download and extract obsidian-full-calender-chronotask-plugin_0.10.8.7z in your vault under .obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/ directory.

4. Enjoy!
releases.name: v0.11.2

releases.author: YouFoundJK
releases.description:
### What’s New in v0.11.1

* **Category-Based Event Coloring**
  Events can now be tagged with structured categories and color-coded for better visual organization.

* **New Data Model**
  Event titles are parsed into `category` and clean `title` fields, improving downstream logic and consistency.
* **Improved Event Modal**
  Redesigned UI with separate inputs for category and title, better layout, and smoother user experience.
* **Bulk Categorization Options**
  Choose how to categorize existing events when enabling category coloring: smart folder-based update, full overwrite, or single default category.
* **Custom Category Colors**
  Events display with category-specific colors when enabled in settings.
* **Better Type Safety & Test Coverage**
  Fixed type errors, aligned parsers with schema, and added comprehensive tests to prevent regressions.
* **Backwards Compatible**
  Category parsing and coloring only apply if explicitly enabled—no surprises for existing users.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/compare/v0.10.13...v0.11.1

---

### Usage
1. Download and extract obsidian-full-calender-chronotask-plugin_0.10.8.7z in your vault under .obsidian/plugins/obsidian-full-calender/ directory.
2. If you prefer the original feel and look of Chronotask, replace the extracted styles.css with styles_original.css!
3. Finally Open the Full-Calender-plugin and you should see an Analysis button on the top right - use it to access Chronotask; Or open the plugin settings to see latest options.
4. Enjoy!

## What's Changed
* Long standing Time Zone issues now solved - if you are a nomad or you are loading remote calender, you should be happy now.
* Display can handle a different timezone for your convenience! (Check Plugin Settings)
* Daily Notes can be strict wrt to TimeZones or Flexible - stays the same relative to current timezone! (Check Plugin Settings)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/YouFoundJK/plugin-full-calendar/commits/v0.10.13
